note: Frozen sanitized future-task cards for the leaf pilot. Authored from the episode's CODE DIFF only (builder provenance) BEFORE any O0 (masked leaf spec body) was read. The executor prompt embeds ONLY the `request` — never the episode sha/subject/commit-message/diff. `abcStratum` (identifiability: A=artifact-identifiable, B=original-only latent, C=truly-new) is frozen here from the PRE-STATE ARTIFACTS + the behavioural need, not from O0. `acceptance` is the hidden external-scorer contract, evaluated on the HOST over the archived workspace by a REAL behavioural test (never regex-as-main). Change = re-registration (commit this file; tasks.json pins its sha256).

spexcode/spec-cli/source-of-truth/spec-l.abcRationale: The C0-era artifacts contain no hint of a git-tracked-enumeration or testGlobs intent — the pre-state lint does an fs-walk with a hardcoded skip list and there is nothing in the snapshot that DEMANDS the change. By the frozen A/B/C definition (A=artifact-identifiable, B=original-only latent, C=truly-new) this is a NEW REQUIREMENT: C. Consequence — this task can only measure NEW-REQUIREMENT practical utility across the arms; it does NOT test reconstruction fidelity (there is no C0 artifact truth to reconstruct). Classified from artifacts, never from O0.
spexcode/spec-cli/source-of-truth/spec-l.provenance: leaf-governed change is scope-self-contained (only lint.ts + spexcode.json among source changed) and replayable (imports ./git, ./specs resolve at preState).
spexcode/spec-cli/source-of-truth/spec-l.request:
This project's spec-governance linter has a coverage check that flags source files under the configured governedRoots which no spec node claims. It currently enumerates candidate source files by WALKING THE FILESYSTEM under each governed root, skipping a small hardcoded set of directories. Two problems: (1) an fs walk under a broad root (e.g. ".") pulls in untracked files, build output, and nested/linked worktrees that aren't real product source; (2) test files get enumerated and demanded to be governed, even though tests aren't governed product.

Change the coverage enumeration so that: it draws source from GIT-TRACKED files only (so untracked files, gitignored build output, and nested/linked worktrees are naturally excluded and a governedRoots of "." becomes safe with no hand-maintained skip list); and files matching a configurable set of test globs (default "**/*.test.*") are excluded from coverage. Keep flagging tracked, non-test, ungoverned source files exactly as before. Make the change in the linter source and its config defaults.

assertions: An UNTRACKED source file under a governed root is NOT reported by coverage (git-tracked-only enumeration).
A file matching the default test glob (**/*.test.*) is NOT reported by coverage.
A tracked, non-test, ungoverned source file IS still reported by coverage (the rule still fires).
acceptance.controls: positive = the real post-episode lint.ts (must pass all); negative = the pre-state fs-walk lint.ts (must be REJECTED — it flags the untracked + test file). Discrimination proven by scorer.selftest.mjs (rc0) before any paid run.
acceptance.boundedScope: (I) These three assertions probe the governed behaviour bounded to lint.ts + spexcode.json; they do NOT exhaustively cover every downstream lint rule interaction. Honest limitation, not a completeness claim. The task's governed set includes the non-source artifact spexcode.json (config), which the scope check treats as a required governed artifact.

spexcode/spec-dashboard/dashboard-ui/mob.abcRationale: Pre-state App.jsx has both a periodic poll and an on-demand reload calling loadBoard().then(setBoard) with no ordering guard — the stale-overwrite race is latent and identifiable directly in the artifact. Artifact-identifiable.
spexcode/spec-dashboard/dashboard-ui/mob.provenance: mobile-ui governs the shared App.jsx; the earlier focus-panel episodes were excluded (new-sibling-module dep / non-governed FocusPanel.jsx source). db80b33d changes ONLY App.jsx among source — scope-self-contained. It is an async board-poll race, so its honest acceptance is a REAL browser/DOM behavioural test.
spexcode/spec-dashboard/dashboard-ui/mob.request: In the dashboard's root App component, the board is refreshed both by a periodic poll (every few seconds) and on demand (after actions like closing or renaming a session). Because several board fetches can be in flight at once and resolve OUT OF ORDER, an older fetch — carrying a stale backend snapshot from before an action — can land last and overwrite fresher state: e.g. a just-closed session flickers back until the next poll. Fix the refresh so that only the MOST-RECENTLY-ISSUED fetch may update the board; a superseded (older-issued) response must be dropped, never applied. Change only the app component.

assertions: SINGLE-REFRESH (independent one-request driver): one normal board refresh updates the rendered board — its sessions appear. A never-updates implementation fails this.

RACE latest-issued-wins: when two refreshes overlap and the LATER-issued resolves first while the EARLIER-issued resolves last with stale data, the rendered board reflects the later-issued (fresh) result.
RACE stale-dropped: the superseded (older-issued) response is NOT applied — the stale session does not appear.
acceptance.drivers: the SINGLE-REFRESH assertion is measured by its OWN one-request driver (mount reload only, resolve once); the two RACE assertions come from the race driver. The single result of the race is not triple-counted.
acceptance.sandbox: IMPLEMENTED — esbuild bundle + headless chromium run inside docker --network none (produced/bundle read-only, only profile/tmp writable, no HOME/checkout mount); network additionally cut in-process via CDP. Positive control (post db80b33d) passes; negative controls: pre-state 3f07397f (no seq-guard → stale wins) AND a never-updates stub both REJECTED. Discrimination proven by pilot check (rc0) before any paid run.
